payload.title: Unified Engine: no-ui-slider-single-pointer — A slider should be operated with a single pointer
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `no-ui-slider-single-pointer`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer`, `/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer_success`, `/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/no-ui-slider-single-pointer/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A slider should be operable with a single pointer
**Remediation advice:** Ensure that the slider can be operated with a single pointer. For example by using the \
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 1 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `no-ui-slider-single-pointer` examples published at `/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer_success` and `/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 2 pass, 1 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

steps.action:
Action:
1. **Step 1 — Open rule detail page in RAIDEN**
2. Navigate to `/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer` in the Engine Rules Library.
3. Verify rule title, description, WCAG references, and impact (`serious`) are displayed.
4. Confirm navigation breadcrumbs show Engine Rules → no-ui-slider-single-pointer.
steps.expected_result:
Expected Result:
1. Rule detail page loads without errors.
2. Title and description match engine-rules metadata.
3. WCAG references and remediation advice are visible when present.
steps.data:
Data:
BASE_URL/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer
ruleId = no-ui-slider-single-pointer
impact = serious

steps.action:
Action:
1. **Step 2 — Review compliant (pass) examples**
2. Navigate to `/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer_success`.
3. For each pass fixture listed in Data, locate the matching HTML example card.
4. Verify each example is labeled compliant and HTML matches the atomic fixture.
steps.expected_result:
Expected Result:
1. Success page loads with COMPLIANT status chip.
2. Each pass fixture from atomic-tests/pass/ is represented as an example.
3. Example HTML content matches the corresponding atomic test file.

steps.action:

1. **Step 3 — Review non-compliant (fail) examples**
2. Navigate to `/engine/no-ui-slider-single-pointer_failure`.
3. For each fail fixture listed in Data, locate the matching HTML example card.
4. Verify each example is labeled needs-fix and HTML matches the atomic fixture.
steps.expected_result:

2. Each fail fixture from atomic-tests/fail/ is represented as an example.
3. Example HTML content matches the corresponding atomic test file.

steps.action:

1. **Step 4 — Run atomic auditor on each fixture**
2. For each fixture in Data, run the core-engine atomic test harness.
3. Assert `response.passed === true` for pass fixtures and `false` for fail fixtures.
4. Verify passedNodes/failedNodes match testconfig expect blocks.

1. All pass fixtures return response.passed = true.
2. All fail fixtures return response.passed = false.
3. Node-level expect.passedNodes / expect.failedNodes assertions pass.

steps.action:

2. Host pass and fail fixture HTML on a test webpage (or use RAIDEN example URLs).
3. Run an AccessFlow scan on the page with rule `no-ui-slider-single-pointer` enabled.
4. Compare generated issues against RAIDEN fail examples; pass examples must not raise false positives.
steps.expected_result:

1. AccessFlow scan completes successfully.
2. Issues for fail fixtures match rule title and affected nodes from atomic tests.
3. No issues raised for pass-only scenarios (no false positives).

payload.title: Unified Engine: page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh — Pages should not use <meta http-equiv="refresh"> for automatic redirection or reloading
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh`, `/engine/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh_success`, `/engine/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A <meta> element with http-equiv="refresh" is sometimes used to automatically redirect users after a time delay. These timed changes can interrupt and disorient users who rely on assistive technology
**Remediation advice:** Remove <meta> elements with http-equiv="refresh". If timed user sessions are necessary, ensure users can extend the session time limit.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 11 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh` examples published at `/engine/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh_success` and `/engine/page-no-meta-http-equiv-refresh_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 4 pass, 11 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: page-title-valid — Page title should be descriptive
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `page-title-valid`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/page-title-valid`, `/engine/page-title-valid_success`, `/engine/page-title-valid_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/page-title-valid/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers rely heavily on page titles to announce the purpose of a page. If titles aren’t descriptive, users with low or no vision may not understand the context until they start navigating the page.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure the title element inside the <head> is unique and describes the purpose of the page.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 1 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `page-title-valid` examples published at `/engine/page-title-valid_success` and `/engine/page-title-valid_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 2 pass, 1 fail atomic HTML files.

3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.
